fidelity.reason: The passage assigns the adaptation decision to an outside observer who cannot make it instead of the business-unit leader.
evidence_spans: an outside observer who cannot make the decision chooses

managerial_framing.reason: It otherwise identifies the adaptation choice, local constraints, enabling mechanism, and imitation-versus-fit trade-off.
evidence_spans: Closer imitation preserves tested elements but may fit the receiving unit poorly

scholarly_positioning.reason: This passage functions as phenomenon motivation, not a scholarly-positioning claim.

evidence_discipline.reason: The scope is limited to observable routines and universal causality is expressly not claimed.

prose_clarity.reason: The narrative is readable and specific.
evidence_spans: Transfer succeeds when the receiving unit recreates the complementary routines

overall_reason: A nondecision-maker is incorrectly made the decision actor.
